target.title: fix(telegram): bind bot mentions to assistant identity
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram group/topic turns where an explicit @bot-handle mention could still be interpreted as addressing someone other than the current assistant, allowing a run to execute tools and then end as `NO_REPLY`. The patch carries explicit bot mention metadata into shared inbound context and adds a Telegram-only prompt line binding the configured bot handle to the assistant identity.

This PR is intentionally scoped to the bot-handle identity slice. It does not claim to close the broader linked Telegram reply-loss cluster.

## Linked context

Related openclaw/openclaw#85654. Live before-fix repro evidence: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85654#issuecomment-4703305519.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic messages explicitly addressed to the configured bot handle are represented as addressed to the current assistant, producing a visible reply rather than `NO_REPLY`.
- Real environment tested: PR branch `b819faf00ebaa65b6e0b6ef8b92aabedd4119c40` running as a local staging OpenClaw gateway on port `18794`, isolated state root `~/.local/state/openclaw-staging/pr93088-proof`, Telegram staging bot `@kesslerClawBot`, staging supergroup `-1003908474243`, topic `1`.
- Exact test message sent in Telegram group/topic: `@kesslerClawBot reply exactly: PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK`.
- Redacted gateway evidence: gateway received Telegram update `471279866` with `chat.type="supergroup"`, `chat.is_forum=true`, text redacted, and a mention entity at offset `0` length `15`.
- Redacted runtime context evidence from the PR branch session:
  - `sessionKey`: `agent:niemand:telegram:group:-1003908474243:topic:1`
  - `chat_id`: `telegram:-1003908474243`
  - `topic_id`: `1`
  - `is_forum`: `true`
  - `is_group_chat`: `true`
  - `was_mentioned`: `true`
  - `explicitly_mentioned_bot`: `true`
  - `mention_source`: `explicit_bot`
- Redacted after-fix session output:
  - user: `@kesslerClawBot reply exactly: PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK`
  - assistant model output: `PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK`
  - OpenClaw delivery mirror: `🤖 PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK`
- Observed result: the PR branch produced a visible Telegram group/topic reply for an explicit bot-handle request. The session artifact contains no `NO_REPLY` final answer for this turn.
- Scope note: this proves only the bot-handle identity slice in this PR. It does not claim the broader Telegram reply-loss cluster is fully fixed.

## Tests validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `gh pr checks 93088 --repo openclaw/openclaw --json name,state,workflow,link` -> 165 checks, `bad: []`

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only Telegram prompts and context metadata for explicit bot-handle mentions.
- Config, environment, migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: prompt wording in group chats. Mitigation: Telegram-gated prompt text, structured mention facts, retained `NO_REPLY` policy, tests for explicit bot mentions versus non-Telegram behavior, and staging Telegram group/topic proof.

## Current review state

Local validation is ready and redacted staging Telegram group/topic proof is now supplied. Broader Telegram reply-loss remains out of scope.

---
